name: Roborock Qrevo S Pro Robot Vacuum and Mop
tag: Best Mid-Range Robot Vacuum Mop with Hot-Water Dock

specs.Dock Functions: Auto dust empty (65-day bag), hot-water mop wash at 75°C, warm-air dry at 45°C, auto water refill

whatWorks: The 75°C hot-water mop wash is the most-cited upgrade over cheaper dock models — owners note grease, pet-oil residue, and kitchen-floor grime clear more effectively than with cold-water competitors. This is the feature that separates the S Pro tier from the QV 35A.
18,500Pa suction handles hard floors convincingly. Oatmeal, rice, coffee beans, and loose debris come up in reviews as single-pass cleanup without requiring multiple runs.

The all-rubber anti-tangle brush gets consistent credit in Reddit threads — long hair and pet fur are guided to brush ends rather than wrapping, eliminating the most common maintenance task on older robot vacuums.
Roborock's app is cited by nearly every reviewer as the most polished in the category — SmartPlan 2.0, per-room settings, and Thorough+ mode are practical differentiators owners actually use daily.
worthKnowing: Side brush cannot extend past the robot body — a consistent note in detailed reviews — leaving a strip along baseboards and furniture legs that requires manual attention. The Qrevo Curv's FlexiArm extension solves this.
On higher-pile carpet, suction numbers don't fully translate to pickup depth. Debris residue on medium-to-high pile is a real-world limitation despite the 18,500Pa rating.
Price can fluctuate on Amazon.ca — verify current pricing before purchase. Confirmed at approximately $749.99 CAD from Roborock CA promotional pricing as of research date.

commonComplaints.title: Corner and edge cleaning comes up…
commonComplaints.body: Corner and edge cleaning comes up repeatedly as a gap — neither the side brush nor the mop modules extend beyond the robot's footprint, and owners in detailed reviews note that baseboards and furniture edges require follow-up hand-cleaning.

commonComplaints.title: Obstacle detection with Reactive Tech struggles…
commonComplaints.body: Obstacle detection with Reactive Tech struggles with flat or small obstacles — thin cables were run over in hands-on testing, and owners consistently note that dedicated no-go zones or pre-cleaning is needed to avoid this.

commonComplaints.title: High-pile carpet performance is a recurring…
commonComplaints.body: High-pile carpet performance is a recurring point of caution — reviewers who tested on taller pile found noticeably more residue than on hard surfaces, and some owners raise it as a reason the Qrevo Curv may be worth the step-up.

verdict: Owners who run a hard-floor-heavy home and want a fully automated, hygienically clean dock without flagship pricing consistently land on the Qrevo S Pro as their recommendation. The 75°C mop wash, anti-tangle brush, and polished app are the three features that come up most in real owner write-ups. The step-up to the Qrevo Curv gets recommended when owners have significant carpet, high pet traffic, or want edge-cleaning coverage.

realBuyers.quote: The hot water cleaning on the dock is the reason I bought this over the cheaper one. You can actually smell the difference after a kitchen run.
Navigation is precise — it went into spots I didn't expect and cleaned under furniture it barely fit under. Not once stuck in two months.
App is genuinely the best I've used. Per-room settings, the Thorough+ mode, SmartPlan — it actually changes how I use it week to week.

metaDescription: Roborock Qrevo S Pro review for Canadian buyers: 18,500Pa suction, 75°C hot-water mop wash, anti-tangle brush, 65-day auto-empty dock. Real owner feedback and Canadian price.
introText: The Roborock Qrevo S Pro is a 2025 upper-mid-range robot vacuum with 18,500Pa HyperForce suction and a dock that washes mop pads at 75°C — the feature that separates this tier from cheaper dock models. It sits above the QV 35A in Roborock's lineup, adding hot-water mop cleaning for hard-floor households that want hands-off daily maintenance without flagship pricing.
